trigger: Building a cache derived from user-event data (e.g. session transcripts, keystrokes, prompts)
rule: Default to event-driven (kqueue/fsevents/inotify) over periodic polling. Cache writes happen ONLY when source data changes, not on a fixed timer. Polling is acceptable as a fallback when the platform lacks event APIs, but never the first choice. Apply to: session transcripts, prompt buffers, file-derived caches in ~/.claude/cache/.
why: On 2026-05-22 Mukul ran build-session-context.py once, then explicitly said: 'building session context isn't a 1 time operation, it shouldn't run periodically.' The intended model is event-driven — the cache updates when transcripts grow, not every 30s. Misreading this as 'wire into the dashboard's 30s pulse' violates his cost/freshness mental model.

sources.user_correction: On 2026-05-22 Mukul ran build-session-context.py once, then explicitly said: 'building session context isn't a 1 time operation, it shouldn't run periodically.' The intended model is event-driven — the cache updates when transcripts grow, not every 30s. Misreading this as 'wire into the dashboard's 30s pulse' violates his cost/freshness mental model.
